2604.08033.title: IoT-Brain: Grounding LLMs for Semantic-Spatial Sensor Scheduling
2604.08033.paper_summary: Intelligent systems powered by large-scale sensor networks are shifting from predefined monitoring to intent-driven operation, revealing a critical Semantic-to-Physical Mapping Gap. While large language models (LLMs) excel at semantic understanding, existing perception-centric pipelines operate retrospectively, overlooking the fundamental decision of what to sense and when. We formalize this proactive decision as Semantic-Spatial Sensor Scheduling (S3) and demonstrate that direct LLM planning is unreliable due to inherent gaps in representation, reasoning, and optimization. To bridge these gaps, we introduce the Spatial Trajectory Graph (STG), a neuro-symbolic paradigm governed by a verify-before-commit discipline that transforms open-ended planning into a verifiable graph optimization problem. Based on STG, we implement IoT-Brain, a concrete system embodiment, and construct TopoSense-Bench, a campus-scale benchmark with 5,250 natural-language queries across 2,510 cameras. Evaluations show that IoT-Brain boosts task success rate by 37.6% over the strongest search-intensive methods while running nearly 2 times faster and using 6.6 times fewer prompt tokens. In real-world deployment, it approaches the reliability upper bound while reducing 4.1 times network bandwidth, providing a foundational framework for LLMs to interact with the physical world with unprecedented reliability and efficiency.
2604.08033.paper_summary_zh: 智能系統由大規模傳感器網絡驅動，正從預定義的監控轉向以意圖驅動的操作，揭示了一個關鍵的語義到物理的映射差距。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在語義理解方面表現出色，但現有的以感知為中心的流程是回顧性的，忽視了感知什麼和何時感知的基本決策。我們將這一主動決策形式化為語義-空間傳感器調度（S3），並證明直接的LLM規劃因表示、推理和優化中的固有差距而不可靠。為了彌補這些差距，我們引入了空間軌跡圖（STG），這是一種神經-符號範式，受驗證-再承諾原則的支配，將開放式規劃轉化為可驗證的圖優化問題。基於STG，我們實現了IoT-Brain，一個具體的系統實體，並構建了TopoSense-Bench，一個擁有5,250個自然語言查詢和2,510個攝像頭的校園規模基準。評估顯示，IoT-Brain的任務成功率比最強的搜索密集方法提高了37.6%，同時運行速度幾乎快了2倍，使用的提示令牌少了6.6倍。在實際部署中，它接近可靠性上限，同時減少了4.1倍的網絡帶寬，為LLMs與物理世界之間的互動提供了前所未有的可靠性和效率的基礎框架。
2604.08033.author: Zhaomeng Zhou et.al.
2604.08033.authors: Zhaomeng Zhou, Lan Zhang, Junyang Wang, Mu Yuan, Junda Lin, Jinke Song
